stories.headline: SEC-CFTC name BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, LINK as digital commodities in joint guidance
stories.tldr: A joint SEC-CFTC interpretation establishes a token taxonomy that explicitly carves the top five names out of securities law.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Years of jurisdictional ambiguity collapse into a single roadmap, clearing runway for spot listings, ETF expansions, and CFTC-regulated derivatives on majors.
the_details: Taxonomy splits assets into digital commodities, collectibles, tools, stablecoins, and securities — most live tokens land outside SEC turf.
SEC Chair Paul Atkins frames the updated MOU as the end of duplicative registration regimes that he says have stifled US innovation.
Guidance lands alongside April's GENIUS Act rulemakings from FDIC, Treasury, FinCEN and OFAC — the full federal stack is finally moving in lockstep.

stories.headline: BTC closes April defensive at $76K as 30-year yield hits 5% and Iran risk lingers
stories.tldr: Bitcoin opened the month's final session at $75,752 — its lowest in over a week — with seasonality flipping bullish for May despite macro headwinds.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A higher-for-longer Fed plus a 5% long-bond yield is the harshest setup for risk assets in years, yet a 50/100-day golden cross is forming — the May tape will test which signal wins.
the_details: ETH tagged $2,220 support after the FOMC, triggering $149.7M in liquidations; BTC longs lost $110M alone.
BTC is still up roughly 13% in April even after the late-month fade, and historical May seasonality plus ETF inflows tilt bullish.
Funding stayed deeply negative while whales added longs — a classic squeeze setup if macro pressure eases into the Fed's June meeting.

stories.headline: April finishes worst crypto-hack month in over a year as bridges and admin keys collapse
stories.tldr: The month closes with roughly $620M drained across 12 incidents, with bridge failures and admin-key compromises dominating the loss column.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The pattern is clear — DeFi's biggest attack surface in 2026 is not smart-contract math, it's centralized operational keys and cross-chain plumbing.
the_details: Drift Protocol ($285M, April 1) and Kelp DAO ($292M, April 19) account for the vast majority of the haul.
Smaller hits piled up: CoW Swap lost $1.2M to a domain hijack, Dango bridge $410K to a contract bug, Silo Finance $392K to a misconfigured oracle.
Wasabi Protocol's late-April $4.5M deployer-key drain underlines the recurring admin-permission failure mode across the month.

stories.headline: Aave Horizon RWA market hits $550M in deposits as tokenized Treasuries go live as collateral
stories.tldr: Aave's institutional-only Horizon market lets qualified entities post tokenized US Treasuries as collateral for stablecoin loans.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This is the cleanest bridge yet between TradFi balance sheets and on-chain liquidity — and it's onboarding real money while DeFi-at-large is still nursing the Kelp wound.
the_details: Horizon's $550M in net deposits is a standout amid Aave's $6B+ TVL drop after the April 19 Kelp exploit.
Tokenized Treasuries serve as collateral for stablecoin borrows, giving institutions on-chain leverage without selling the underlying.
Aave also deployed natively on Solana on April 27 via the Sunrise bridge, extending its multi-chain footprint as it rebuilds confidence.

stories.headline: XRP sentiment rockets into FOMO zone after Rakuten Pay partnership lands
stories.tldr: XRP's social-sentiment ratio spiked into the FOMO band on April 29 as Rakuten Pay confirmed an XRP rails integration in Japan.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A Japanese super-app payments integration is the first credible mainstream consumer rail for XRP since the SEC overhang lifted — adoption optionality is finally re-pricing.
the_details: Rakuten Pay reaches tens of millions of Japanese users, immediately broadening XRP's real-world utility surface.
The pop comes with BTC dominance at 60% and CMC's Altcoin Season Index at just 39/100 — clean alpha against a sleepy alt tape.
Comes days after the SEC-CFTC guidance explicitly listed XRP as a digital commodity, removing the last regulatory cloud.
